starter.label: Starter
starter.summary: Core player dashboard with headline metrics and trends.

coach_pro.label: Coach Pro
coach_pro.summary: Team comparison, training recommendations, session plans, and player reports.

elite.label: Elite
elite.summary: Advanced benchmarking, guided onboarding, and premium report experience.

custom.label: Custom
custom.summary: Configurable deployment for academy and performance environments.

cards.title: What the trend chart shows
cards.body: The trend chart tracks winners, errors, and efficiency over recent matches to show whether performance is stabilising or regressing.
bullets: Use at least 4-6 matches for meaningful direction.
Judge both absolute values and trend direction.
Pair trend data with coaching observations.

cards.title: How plans are generated
cards.body: Session plans use the player's strongest shot, biggest error source, and benchmark gap to build a practical next-session structure.
bullets: Protect confidence by training from a strength.
Keep one tactical scenario block in every session.

cards.title: How insight priority works
cards.body: Strengths reflect efficient winner contribution and repeatability. Weaknesses reflect error burden, tactical exposure, and conversion gaps.
bullets: Do not overreact to one match.
Use insight lists to guide training themes.

cards.title: How gaps are calculated
cards.body: Current performance is compared against a target level profile. Positive gap means above target for positive metrics, while a negative gap means below target.
bullets: Use benchmark priority to sequence coaching focus.

tour_steps.title: Player snapshot
tour_steps.body: Start with the headline, best shot, priority fix, and tracked matches to understand the current story quickly.

tour_steps.title: Trend layer
tour_steps.body: Use the trend chart and signals to spot whether recent work is moving the player in the right direction.

tour_steps.title: Benchmarking
tour_steps.body: Compare current performance against the target profile to decide what matters most next.

tour_steps.title: Squad comparison
tour_steps.body: In team view, compare players side by side to identify who needs what support.

john.headline: Strong forehand base, improving backhand consistency
john.summary: John creates solid forehand pressure and is trending in the right direction, but still leaks points through backhand errors and passive return decisions.

milestones.title: Backhand error rate below 40%
milestones.detail: Down from 46% to 43% over the last 4 matches.

milestones.title: Forehand remains primary point-builder
milestones.detail: Forehand still leads winner contribution and rally control.

recommended_drills.title: Serve + first ball forehand pattern
recommended_drills.goal: Use strength to own the second shot.

blocks.title: Technical warm-up
blocks.detail: Backhand rhythm, spacing, and controlled tempo.

blocks.title: Pressure repeaters
blocks.detail: Backhand under movement with target-zone scoring.

blocks.title: Pattern play
blocks.detail: Build points into forehand finish after safe backhand neutralisation.

blocks.title: Competitive games
blocks.detail: Score-based points starting from return situations.

strengths: Forehand remains the cleanest attacking platform.
Winner production is trending up match to match.
weaknesses: Backhand error burden is still the main leak.
Returns can become too aggressive too early in the rally.
Backhand shape breaks down under pace.
recommendations: Use forehand patterns after safe neutral balls.
tactical_observations: Keep points cross-court longer before redirecting.
Do not force line changes from unstable backhand positions.

jenny.headline: Net game improving, needs better timing and patience
jenny.summary: Jenny is becoming more assertive at the net, but still gives away too many points by closing too early and over-forcing first volleys.

milestones.title: Net approach discipline improving
milestones.detail: Better hold position before advancing on shorter balls.

milestones.title: Volley confidence growing
milestones.detail: Volley winner contribution now matches forehand winner contribution.

recommended_drills.title: Transition stop-starts
recommended_drills.goal: Improve movement timing toward the net.

blocks.title: Movement prep
blocks.detail: Split-step timing and forward movement cues.

blocks.title: Volley control
blocks.detail: First-volley targets with live feeds and scoring.

blocks.title: Backhand reliability
blocks.detail: Cross-court neutral balls with rhythm goals.

blocks.title: Constraint points
blocks.detail: Points where volley winners only count after two safe balls.

strengths: Volley intent is becoming a real weapon.
Recent match winners have increased steadily.
weaknesses: Backhand misses still disrupt neutral rallies.
Approach timing can expose the body too early.

strengths: More willingness to finish when set.
recommendations: Delay net commitment until ball quality allows it.
Train calm first volleys to the middle third.
tactical_observations: Use deeper margin targets before closing.
Win the transition before trying to win the point.

heidi.headline: Defensive stability improving, overhead still a weakness
heidi.summary: Heidi is defending with more control and reading rallies better, but overhead situations still create too many cheap errors and missed finishing chances.

milestones.title: Defensive rally tolerance improving
milestones.detail: Forehand and backhand defence both trending steadier.

milestones.title: Overhead confidence still in build phase
milestones.detail: Error rate is slowly reducing but remains above target.

recommended_drills.title: Overhead footwork entry
recommended_drills.goal: Improve setup and spacing under lobs.

recommended_drills.title: Defend-to-attack game
recommended_drills.goal: Convert good defence into controlled offence.

blocks.title: Footwork prep
blocks.detail: Turn, crossover, and spacing before overhead contact.

blocks.title: Bandeja repetition
blocks.detail: High-margin overhead targets from fed lobs.

blocks.title: Live lob recovery
blocks.detail: Decision making from defensive and neutral lobs.

blocks.title: Scenario points
blocks.detail: Score points where overhead execution is the trigger to attack.

strengths: Defensive control is becoming more repeatable.
Winner count is climbing without extra shot volume spikes.
weaknesses: Overhead error rate remains the largest single weakness.
Missed overhead finishes leave points unfinished.

strengths: Reads slower balls earlier than before.
weaknesses: Overhead setup too late under high lobs.
Needs a safer default overhead pattern.
recommendations: Train a reliable bandeja before chasing winners.
tactical_observations: Respect deep lobs and create space early.
Use safer overhead placement until balance improves.
